Restaurant Inventory Analyzer
  • GUI (Graphical User Interface)
  • View page source

GUI (Graphical User Interface)

main_gui.py

Entry point for the Restaurant Inventory Analyzer desktop application. Provides a Tkinter-based GUI that allows the user to:

  1. Load and clean a raw Excel inventory file

  2. Run various analyses on the cleaned data

  3. Display results as interactive charts or scrollable tables

main_gui.browse() → None[source]

Open a file dialog filtered to .xlsx files and store the selected path.

main_gui.make_btn(text: str, cmd: callable, color: str = '#e94560') → Button[source]

Create a styled sidebar button.

Parameters:

  • text (str — button label)

  • cmd (callable — function to call when clicked)

  • color (str — background hex colour (defaults to ACCENT))

Return type:

tk.Button

main_gui.show_chart(df: DataFrame, x_col: str, y_col: str, title: str, xlabel: str | None = None, ylabel: str | None = None) → None[source]

Render a bar chart inside results_frame. Clears any previously displayed content before drawing.

Parameters:

  • df (pd.DataFrame — data to plot)

  • x_col (str — column name for the x-axis (categories))

  • y_col (str — column name for the y-axis (values))

  • title (str — chart title)

  • xlabel (Optional[str] — optional x-axis label (defaults to x_col))

  • ylabel (Optional[str] — optional y-axis label (defaults to y_col))

main_gui.show_table(df: DataFrame) → None[source]

Render a scrollable table inside results_frame using ttk.Treeview. Clears any previously displayed content before drawing.

Parameters:

df (pd.DataFrame — data to display as a table)

main_gui.run_clean() → None[source]

Read the selected Excel file, run the full cleaning pipeline, and display the cleaned DataFrame as a table. Sets cleaned_df so analysis functions can use it.

main_gui.run_weekday() → None[source]

Compute and display average income per weekday as a bar chart.

main_gui.run_holiday() → None[source]
Compute holiday vs non-holiday earnings and display two side-by-side bar charts:

  • Total earnings comparison

  • Average earnings comparison

main_gui.run_most_popular() → None[source]

Compute and display the top 10 most ordered dishes as a bar chart.

main_gui.run_volume_of_dishes() → None[source]

Compute and display total dish volume per day of the week as a bar chart.

main_gui.run_dish_volume_by_day() → None[source]

Compute and display average order volume per dish per weekday as a table.

main_gui.on_close() → None[source]

Close all matplotlib figures and destroy the window cleanly on exit.

Built with Sphinx using a theme provided by Read the Docs.
